perturbation.original_text: Rogers shall have the non-exclusive right to distribute and exhibit each Licensed Program on a VOD basis for a period of ninety (90) consecutive days, or such longer period as may be agreed to by Rogers and Licensor (the "License Period").
perturbation.changed_text: Rogers shall have the non-exclusive right to distribute and exhibit each Licensed Program on a VOD basis for a period of ninety (90) consecutive days, unless Rogers unilaterally decides to extend the period (the "License Period").
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies the 'License Period' as 90 days or a mutually agreed-upon longer period. The modified text gives Rogers the unilateral right to extend this period, which contradicts the initial condition requiring mutual agreement. This creates uncertainty about the actual duration of the license and gives Rogers unrestricted control over the License Period, conflicting the need for mutual agreement. This contradicts the original condition requiring mutual agreement to extend the license period set forth in Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, Rogers shall have the non-exclusive right to distribute and exhibit each Licensed Program on a VOD basis for a period of ninety (90) consecutive days, unless Rogers unilaterally decides to extend the period (the "License Period").
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for an extension of the 'License Period' only through mutual agreement between Rogers and the Licensor. The changed text, also present in the contradicted text, grants Rogers the unilateral right to extend the period. This creates a direct contradiction because one requires mutual consent, while the other permits unilateral action, making the terms of the contract uncertain and potentially unenforceable. This directly violates the need for mutual agreement to extend the license period.

perturbation.original_text: Licensor shall use commercial reasonable efforts to provide Rogers with Licensed Programs with closed captioning.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensor is not obligated to provide Rogers with Licensed Programs with closed captioning, but any closed captioning costs incurred by Rogers will be deducted from Licensor's License Fees.
perturbation.explanation: The original text indicates that Licensor will use 'commercial reasonable efforts' to provide closed captioning, implying an obligation. The modified text removes the obligation entirely. Additionally, adding that costs for closed captioning incurred by Rogers will be deducted from the Licensor's revenues creates further contradiction. The original text implies the Licensor is providing programs with closed captioning as part of their service, but the change suggests that Licensor has no obligation to do so, but will be penalized if Rogers decides to add it themselves. This in-text contradiction introduces confusion about the obligation for closed captioning and financial liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Notwithstanding Section 6(a) above, (i) Prior to calculating License Fees, Rogers shall be entitled to deduct from Retail Revenues on a rolling monthly basis during the Term ("Rogers Costs"): (A) the value of programming credits, rebates or other retail price discounts provided to ROD Service subscribers in conjunction with a public marketing or promotional initiative; -2- Source: EUROMEDIA HOLDINGS CORP, 10SB12G, 2/15/2007 (B) the value of refunds provided by Rogers to ROD Service subscribers for technical or other errors during such month; (C) the percentage equal to Rogers' average year-to-end bad debt exposure (including credit collection costs) for the then current calendar year in respect of all Rogers' cable television subscribers (expressed as a percentage) (D) encoding costs incurred by Rogers, if applicable, pursuant to Section 8 (a) ; and (E) closed captioning costs incurred by Rogers to comply with Applicable Law.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text implies an obligation to provide closed captioning with 'commercial reasonable efforts'. The modified text explicitly states there is no obligation, but then introduces a financial penalty (deduction from license fees) if Rogers incurs closed captioning costs. This directly contradicts section (b) of the Contradicted Text which allows Rogers to deduct closed captioning costs incurred to comply with Applicable Law, thereby creating a contradiction. The modified text suggests Rogers bears the financial burden even if Licensor has no obligation to provide captioning while the contradicted text states Rogers is entitled to deduct closed captioning costs, thereby creating a direct contradiction.
